dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7348 IH: Transparency in Federal Land Acquisitions Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Arrington (for himself and Mr. Ezell) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To require the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service to provide an opportunity for public notice and comment with respect to land protection plans.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Transparency in Federal Land Acquisitions Act.

section.header: Notice and comment for land protection plans

subsection.text: Notwithstanding section 553(a) of title 5, United States Code, when preparing a land protection plan, the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service shall—

paragraph.text: not less than 60 days before the date on which the land protection plan is finalized, publish the proposed land protection plan in the Federal Register; and
after the publication required by paragraph (1), give interested persons an opportunity to comment on the draft land protection plan through submission of written data, views, or arguments.

subsection.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply with respect to a land protection plan for a proposed expansion to a unit of the National Wildlife Refuge System if such expansion is comprised of a total acreage less than the greater of the following:

paragraph.text: 15 percent of the total acreage of the unit.
